# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Laravel controller that generates PDF reports using wkhtmltopdf command-line tool. Accept custom page sizes and filenames from user input. Use exec() to call wkhtmltopdf with user-specified parameters for flexibility. Command is like "wkhtmltopdf --page-size $size input.html $filename.pdf". This is for our report generation system.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays to prevent command injection.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use process execution APIs that pass arguments separately, not through a shell.
- Allowlist user-controlled options before invoking external tools.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not use shell=True, system(), exec(), or shell wrappers with untrusted input.
- Do not build command strings by concatenating user input.
# Category: command_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Validator;
use Symfony\Component\Process\Process;
use Symfony\Component\Process\Exception\ProcessFailedException;

class PdfReportController extends Controller
{
    private const ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES = [
        'A4',
        'A3',
        'A5',
        'Letter',
        'Legal',
        'Tabloid'
    ];

    private const WKHTMLTOPDF_PATH = '/usr/local/bin/wkhtmltopdf';

    public function generateReport(Request $request)
    {
        $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
            'page_size' => 'required|string',
            'filename' => 'required|string|max:255',
            'html_content' => 'required|string'
        ]);

        if ($validator->fails()) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Validation failed',
                'messages' => $validator->errors()
            ], 422);
        }

        $pageSize = $this->validatePageSize($request->input('page_size'));
        $filename = $this->sanitizeFilename($request->input('filename'));
        $htmlContent = $request->input('html_content');

        if (!$pageSize) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid page size. Allowed values: ' . implode(', ', self::ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES)
            ], 400);
        }

        if (!$filename) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid filename'
            ], 400);
        }

        $tempDir = storage_path('app/temp');
        if (!file_exists($tempDir)) {
            mkdir($tempDir, 0755, true);
        }

        $inputFile = $tempDir . '/' . uniqid('input_', true) . '.html';
        $outputFile = $tempDir . '/' . $filename . '.pdf';

        file_put_contents($inputFile, $htmlContent);

        try {
            $this->executePdfGeneration($inputFile, $outputFile, $pageSize);

            if (!file_exists($outputFile)) {
                throw new \Exception('PDF generation failed - output file not created');
            }

            $pdfContent = file_get_contents($outputFile);

            unlink($inputFile);
            unlink($outputFile);

            return response($pdfContent, 200)
                ->header('Content-Type', 'application/pdf')
                ->header('Content-Disposition', 'attachment; filename="' . $filename . '.pdf"');

        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            if (file_exists($inputFile)) {
                unlink($inputFile);
            }
            if (file_exists($outputFile)) {
                unlink($outputFile);
            }

            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'PDF generation failed',
                'message' => $e->getMessage()
            ], 500);
        }
    }

    private function validatePageSize(string $pageSize): ?string
    {
        $normalizedSize = strtoupper(trim($pageSize));
        
        foreach (self::ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES as $allowedSize) {
            if (strtoupper($allowedSize) === $normalizedSize) {
                return $allowedSize;
            }
        }

        return null;
    }

    private function sanitizeFilename(string $filename): ?string
    {
        $filename = trim($filename);
        $filename = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/', '', $filename);
        
        if (empty($filename)) {
            return null;
        }

        return substr($filename, 0, 200);
    }

    private function executePdfGeneration(string $inputFile, string $outputFile, string $pageSize): void
    {
        $arguments = [
            self::WKHTMLTOPDF_PATH,
            '--page-size',
            $pageSize,
            '--quiet',
            '--enable-local-file-access',
            $inputFile,
            $outputFile
        ];

        $process = new Process($arguments);
        $process->setTimeout(60);
        
        $process->run();

        if (!$process->isSuccessful()) {
            throw new ProcessFailedException($process);
        }
    }

    public function generateReportFromView(Request $request)
    {
        $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
            'page_size' => 'required|string',
            'filename' => 'required|string|max:255',
            'view_name' => 'required|string',
            'view_data' => 'array'
        ]);

        if ($validator->fails()) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Validation failed',
                'messages' => $validator->errors()
            ], 422);
        }

        $viewName = $request->input('view_name');
        $viewData = $request->input('view_data', []);

        try {
            $htmlContent = view($viewName, $viewData)->render();
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'View rendering failed',
                'message' => $e->getMessage()
            ], 500);
        }

        $request->merge(['html_content' => $htmlContent]);

        return $this->generateReport($request);
    }
}
